statement: For every tree T with k edges and maximum degree at least 3 together with every integer m ≥ 2, every sufficiently large n-vertex bipartite graph with more than (k-2)n/2 edges contains a copy of T or an even cycle of length at most 2m, and the unique extremal graphs are precisely the complete bipartite graphs K_{k-2,n-k+2}.

details.subgraph_target: a copy of T or an even cycle of length at most 2m
details.note: Subgraph containment test requires explicit graph description

semantic_scholar.query: tree bipartite cycle bipartite maximum degree

llm_eval.proof_strategy: Combine greedy/DFS-style tree embedding lemmas with dependent random choice or KST-type counting to enforce girth while exceeding the given Turán density.
llm_eval.notes: Edge-count formula appears inconsistent with the stated extremal graph (likely a minor typo). No directly matching theorems located, but the statement sits at the intersection of known sparse-tree and even-cycle extremal problems.

statement: For every integer k ≥ 2 and all sufficiently large n, every maximum-size n-vertex bipartite C_{2k}-free graph is asymptotically equivalent to a known algebraic polarity graph achieving (1/2 + o(1))n^{1+1/k} edges.

details.note: Statement did not match any known testable pattern

llm_eval.proof_strategy: Combine the KST upper bound with a stability argument that forces any extremal example to be close in edit distance to a finite-geometry polarity graph.
llm_eval.notes: For k=2 the statement reduces to a known result of Füredi; for k>2 both the exact leading constant 1/2 and the uniqueness claim remain open. No counter-examples or confirming constructions beyond the polarity graphs are recorded in the literature.

statement: For every integer k ≥ 2 and all sufficiently large n, every maximum-size n-vertex C_{2k}-free graph is asymptotically equivalent to a known algebraic polarity graph achieving (1/2 + o(1))n^{1+1/k} edges.

details.note: Statement did not match any known testable pattern

llm_eval.proof_strategy: Apply stability methods (e.g., graphon or counting lemma techniques) to show that any extremal C_{2k}-free graph must be asymptotically close in edit distance to an algebraic polarity graph.
llm_eval.notes: Generalizes known C_4 results but higher even cycles lack exact extremal characterizations. Algebraic constructions achieve the conjectured density, yet uniqueness/stability remains open. No directly contradicting literature surfaced.
